concept: Pace / Rate and Rhythm at Which an Activity Proceeds
description: The rate and rhythm at which an activity, exchange, or sequence proceeds. Pace can change within one event and can be too fast, too slow, or uneven for a stated participant and purpose. 'thimu' (time) names the broader dimension; 'thimelo' names how movement is distributed through it.
sound_symbolism: Bright 'thi' sends a fine breath past the teeth. Stressed 'me' closes softly at the lips, then final 'lo' releases the tongue into round 'o'. At its center the sound pauses, then opens into a low liquid ending.
grammatical_notes: A noun often modified by speed or comparison words: 'ha thimelo sheru nai.' (This pace is slow.) A possessor may name the meeting, task, speech, or route whose pace is being discussed. Distinct from 'thimora' (schedule) and 'lumethi' (deadline).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Attention has a pace; forcing every perception or decision into the same interval can hide what slower notice would reveal.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Naming pace makes speed negotiable instead of treating the fastest speaker or process as the natural clock for everyone.

semantic_domains.activity: rate and rhythm of a continuing action
semantic_domains.dialogue: speed at which an exchange proceeds
